title: Pod Security, Security Contexts, Images, Admission, Secrets, and Multi-Tenancy · Stakeholder Platform Mission
summary: A legal-aid document processing team. The team needs scoped identity, restricted Pod security, immutable images, secrets protection, network and storage isolation, admission evidence, fault containment, and an honest residual-risk statement. Planned artifact: Deliver Least-Privilege Tenant Workload Boundary with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Detect bind, escalate, impersonate, service account, secret, workload-creation, node-proxy, CSR, aggregation, and wildcard privilege escalation paths.
Apply Privileged, Baseline, and Restricted Pod Security Standards with enforce, audit, warn, version pinning, exemptions, namespace labels, and rollout evidence.
Set non-root identity, groups, filesystem ownership, read-only root, capabilities, privilege escalation, seccomp, host namespaces, devices, and writable mounts from threat evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Project K8s Rbac Escalation Aggregation
steps.instruction:
project case — A project Pod runs as root, is privileged, adds capabilities, mounts hostPath, permits privilege escalation, and bypasses a namespace policy exception. Enforce Pod Security, securityContext, seccomp, admission, exception ownership, and residual shared-kernel risk.

steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-multitenancy-isolation-defense-1b64v0". Tailor its restricted-workload-policy.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-multitenancy-isolation-defense-1b64v0
  - competency: k8s-multitenancy-isolation-defense
    artifact: restricted-workload-policy.yaml
    decision: multitenancy isolation defense
    environment: high-latency edge cluster with intermittent control-plane access
    fault-injection: drain a node while the disruption budget has no headroom
    retained-proof: authorization review token audience and audit correlation
    case-key: 1b64v0
    change: |
      pod-security: restricted-v1.36
      runAsNonRoot: true
      allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
      capabilities-drop: ALL
      seccompProfile: RuntimeDefault
      readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
      image: digest-and-signature-required
      admission: fail-closed-with-breakglass
    verify: kubectl auth can-i create pods -n intake && kubectl apply --server-side --dry-run=server -f restricted.yaml
    failure: Reject removed PodSecurityPolicy, namespace-only multi-tenancy, mutable images, privileged host access, broad secrets, or unavailable admission without a plan.
    repair: Enforce versioned Pod Security, minimize container and API authority, isolate network and storage, verify supply chain, and test break-glass recovery.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

Competency probe: Combine namespaces, RBAC, quotas, network policy, storage classes, scheduling isolation, Pod security, admission, audit, and dedicated clusters according to trust.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Combine namespaces, RBAC, quotas, network policy, storage classes, scheduling isolation, Pod security, admission, audit, and dedicated clusters according to trust.
